commit.message:
Add Agency Locations and Location-Level Mergers

Introduces AgencyLocation and PersonnelLocation entities, moving all
address/contact fields off Agency onto dedicated location records. Every
agency has 1+ locations; exactly one must be flagged IsCorporateOffice.

Backend changes:
- Domain: AgencyLocation, PersonnelLocation entities; MergerType enum
- Merger/MergerParticipant extended with MergerType and AbsorbedLocationId
- AgencyConfiguration removes address fields; AgencyLocationConfiguration added
- IAgencyLocationRepository + AgencyLocationRepository implementation
- IUnitOfWork/UnitOfWork extended with AgencyLocations repository
- AgencyLocationsController: full CRUD + set-corporate + personnel assignments
- AgenciesController: maps Locations[], CorporateStateCode; InitialLocation on create
- MergersController: POST /mergers/location for location acquisition; Execute
  branches on MergerType to handle location re-parenting and personnel transfer
- AgencyRepository includes Locations in all queries; state filter via locations
- MergerRepository includes AbsorbedLocation in participant includes
- EF migration AddLocationEntities adds AgencyLocations, PersonnelLocations tables,
  MergerType column on Mergers, AbsorbedLocationId on MergerParticipants

Frontend changes:
- api.models.ts: AgencyLocation, PersonnelLocation interfaces; MergerType enum;
  Agency loses address fields, gains locations[] and corporateStateCode
- api.service.ts: all agency-location CRUD methods + createLocationMerger
- agency-detail.component.ts: Locations tab with add/edit/delete, set-corporate,
  personnel assignment/removal; profile tab shows corporate office address
- agency-form.component.ts: removes address fields; adds optional initial location
  section for create mode
- merger-wizard.component.ts: merger type toggle (Agency vs Location); Location
  flow: acquiring agency + location picker; step 2 shows location preview
- merger-detail.component.ts: shows mergerType; Location mergers show acquired
  location name instead of absorbed agency

commit.message:
Fix ghost pages in PDF by saving/restoring cursor position

The root cause: pdfkit's doc.save()/restore() only preserves graphics
state, not cursor position (doc.x/doc.y). During switchToPage() loops,
doc.text() and doc.image() in header/footer advance the cursor past
the bottom margin, triggering auto-pagination and creating empty pages.

Fix: manually save and restore doc.x/doc.y around all header/footer
rendering on each buffered page.

Also increase DOCX logo from 160x40 to 220x55 for better visibility.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

Add browser fingerprinting headers and cookie session to bypass eBay bot detection

eBay was blocking requests from Render's datacenter IPs due to minimal HTTP
headers. Now collects session cookies on startup and sends full Chrome-like
headers (Sec-Ch-Ua, Sec-Fetch-*, DNT, etc.) with every request. Cookies are
refreshed on rate-limit retries.

Add F1-macro to full evaluation pipeline (Metal → Rust → Python)

Metal shaders now write predicted_cluster per example to a new output
buffer. Rust compute_f1_macro() builds confusion matrix from predictions
and returns macro-averaged F1 alongside CE and accuracy. Return type
propagated through all 13 files: eval_worker, adaptive, ids_cache,
token_cache, multistage, neighbor_search, lib.rs (PyO3), and Python
evaluators (EvalResult.f1_macro field).

commit.message:
Fix module load order: virtio_pmem must load before virtio_pci

modules-load.d files are processed alphabetically. virtio-pmem.conf
sorted after nixos.conf (which loads virtio_pci), so virtio_pci probed
the PCI bus before virtio_pmem registered its driver. The pmem PCI
device (1af4:105b) was enabled but had no matching virtio driver.

Rename to 00-virtio-pmem.conf to ensure virtio_pmem loads first.

commit.message:
Add SentinelCapture unit tests and pre-commit test hook

Comprehensive vitest test suite for the sentinel state machine covering
boot sequence, command capture, display output, skip/mute mechanics,
freeze/unfreeze, and the seed→lesson transition race condition.

Pre-commit hook runs `npm test` to catch regressions before commits.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

fix: 72 bugs across 9 audit passes — security, data integrity, runtime stability

Security (CRITICAL/HIGH):
- Path traversal in SPA fallback (arbitrary file read)
- Cross-tenant data leak in contacts, WhatsApp, GHL conversations
- Auth bypass in webhook_management, api_keys, conversation_results
- SSRF protection for API integrations, MCP servers, webhooks
- PostgREST filter injection in public API and contacts search
- Empty API key scopes granting full access
- Double encryption of BYOK keys on agent update
- Stripe webhook idempotency (prevent double credit)
- CORS regex restricting attacker subdomains
- Evolution webhook signature validation (HMAC)
- GHL API key masked in responses
- Open redirect prevention in Billing checkout

Runtime stability:
- 6 agent crash risks (None access, missing .get(), bare exceptions)
- Sync DB calls blocking event loop (config_loader, schedule_tool)
- Fire-and-forget task GC prevention (session_handler, webhook_service, outbound_service, main)
- LiveKitAPI connection leak on error (try/finally)
- Lock memory leak cap in WhatsApp/GHL services
- Rejection session with TTS so callers hear the message
- Widget token TTL (10 min expiry)
- JWKS cache with 1-hour TTL (key rotation support)
- Circuit breaker fallback provider chains

Data integrity:
- Campaign credit check before start
- Atomic campaign status transitions
- CRM tool preserves original contact source on upsert
- Phone normalization with country code support
- Calendar DST timezone handling
- Quality score bucket gaps fixed
- Dashboard overview scoped + limited queries

Frontend:
- Settings.jsx useToast destructuring fix
- api.js 204 No Content handling
- Contacts search resets to page 1
- QualityMonitor empty list display

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

fix: Await Slack notifications to prevent serverless function early termination

Critical fix: The onboarding notifications were being cut off because the
serverless function was terminating before the async Slack API call completed.

Root cause: Using .then().catch() without await meant the function returned
the HTTP response and terminated, killing the pending Slack API call.

Solution: Changed both routes to use await with try/catch blocks. This ensures:
1. Slack notification completes before function terminates
2. Proper error handling and logging
3. User still gets success response even if Slack fails

This explains why:
- We saw "📤 Sending Slack message" log
- But never saw success or error logs after
- Payload was prepared but response never came back

🤖 Generated with [Claude Code](https://claude.com/claude-code)

Initial commit: Okta AI Agent webapp with ID-JAG token exchange

A production-ready web application implementing Okta AI Agent authentication
with ID-JAG (Identity Assertion JWT Authorization Grant) token exchange.

Features:
- Okta SSO authentication via ORG authorization server
- AI Agent identity with private_key_jwt (RS256)
- ID-JAG token exchange per IETF draft spec
- Token viewer showing user tokens and ID-JAG token
- Environment-based configuration (no hardcoded secrets)
- Comprehensive documentation of token flow
- Integration with NIST CSF 2.0 MCP server

Architecture:
- Users authenticate via webapp client (Web App type)
- AI Agent exchanges user ID token for ID-JAG token at ORG server
- ID-JAG token contains dual identity (user + agent)
- Agent uses ID-JAG to call MCP server on behalf of user

Technology Stack:
- Express.js with Passport OIDC
- JWT Bearer assertions (RS256)
- Session management
- Modern dark-themed UI

Documentation includes:
- Complete token dance explanation
- When to use webapp client ID vs agent ID
- ORG server vs custom server usage
- Step-by-step authentication flow
- Environment variable setup guide

Clean repository - no secrets in code or history.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

commit.message:
feat: v19 Phase 2 evaluation metrics + deepinit AGENTS.md

## v19 Phase 1 (파이프라인 개선) — 전체 실패, baseline 유지
- Step 2 mode-based vocal_center: mode pitch가 서브하모닉 자체 → REVERTED
- Step 3 confidence filter: 전곡 밀도 < 4.0 n/s, 미작동 → REVERTED
- Step 4 onset strength weighting: FP 증가, mel_strict -27% → REVERTED
- Step 5 multi-scale diatonic: 중립/악화 → REVERTED
- 결론: mel_strict 병목 = FCPE 하모닉 혼동(±2-7st), 후처리로 해결 불가

## v19 Phase 2 (평가 메트릭 개선)
- core/comparator.py: mel_strict_oct(옥타브 무관 50ms), pitch_accuracy_at_onset,
  rhythm_similarity, perceptual_score 추가
- core/reference_extractor.py: contour-following method 추가 (method="contour"),
  skyline이 기본값 유지 (contour는 skyline 대비 열위 확인)
- scripts/evaluate.py: 새 메트릭 출력 + --ref-method 플래그

## 평가 결과 (8곡 avg, FCPE mode)
- mel_strict: 0.091 (baseline 유지)
- mel_strict_oct: 0.109 (+20%, 옥타브 오류 ~18%)
- perceptual_score: 0.537

## 새 스크립트
- scripts/ablation_test.py: 단일 곡 빠른 ablation 테스트
- scripts/diagnose.py: 곡별 오류 분류 진단

## AGENTS.md 계층 문서화 (deepinit)
- 9개 디렉토리에 AGENTS.md 생성 (root, core, scripts, test, output, results + 서브)
- 절대 명제 5가지, 아키텍처 가드레일, 실험 이력 포함

commit.message:
Wire ontology clustering into API and add two-pass extraction (#31, #32)

#31: Ontology clustering
- Add POST /admin/ontology/cluster endpoint with level, threshold,
  dry_run parameters
- Wire AdminService.cluster_ontology() to build_entity/type/rel_type
  clusters with apply support
- Add apply_clusters() to write canonical labels back to DB and
  store cluster definitions in new ontology_clusters table
- Migration 008: ontology_clusters table, canonical_type on nodes,
  canonical_rel_type on edges, cluster_id FK on nodes
- Graph sidecar reload uses COALESCE(canonical_*, raw) so search
  naturally uses clustered ontology

#32: Two-pass entity extraction
- Add TwoPassExtractor: GLiNER for entities (fast, local), targeted
  LLM for relationships only (cheaper, less hallucination)
- Add relationship-only prompt (SYSTEM_PROMPT_RELATIONSHIPS) and
  extract_relationships() method on LlmExtractor
- Falls back to single-pass LLM if GLiNER sidecar unavailable
- Skips LLM pass entirely when GLiNER finds no entities (~20-30%
  of chunks)
- Config: COVALENCE_ENTITY_EXTRACTOR=two_pass activates it
- Validates LLM relationship output against GLiNER entity names

commit.message:
fix: Add input escaping and enhanced error logging for Slack notifications

Root cause analysis: Onboarding notifications were failing silently due to
unescaped special characters in user input breaking Slack API calls.

Fixes:
1. Added escapeSlackText() function to escape &, <, > characters
2. Applied escaping to all user-generated content (email, comments, channels, etc.)
3. Enhanced error logging:
   - Log payload size before sending
   - Log full error details and stack traces
   - Log failed payloads for debugging

This resolves the issue where onboarding notifications were attempted but
never completed (no success or error logs).

🤖 Generated with [Claude Code](https://claude.com/claude-code)

fix: kill session on Retry and escalate on CRITICAL state update failures

Two P1 fixes from #90:

1. Retry() now calls CleanupSession before clearing CurrentSession,
   preventing the old tmux session from running alongside the retry.

2. handleIdleSession fix-round and handleExitedSession now escalate
   (return error action) when store.Update fails instead of silently
   continuing with inconsistent state. The fix-round path also cleans
   up the orphaned session.

Refs #90

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

Location-based recording consent with audit trail

Detect user's state via expo-location to show appropriate consent flow:
- Two-party states (CA, FL, IL, etc.): require explicit checkbox confirmation
- One-party states: softer recommendation with "don't show again" option
- Unknown/no location: defaults to two-party (safer)

Consent metadata (type, state, allPartiesConfirmed, timestamp) stored on
each visit document for audit. Requires new binary build for location
permission; gracefully falls back to two-party until then.

identified by security audit.

- camera_topology.html: 7 locations (same patterns)

- sync_settings.html: sync history and status messages
- app.js: error messages and video URLs
- camera-sync.js: empty state title/message
- prediction-review-mobile.js: search text in innerHTML

repository.name: truckflow-crm
repository.description: TruckFlow CRM - Trucking Operations Management Platform

commit.message:
Add Gmail MCP — end-to-end OAuth, MCP protocol endpoint, and dashboard UI

- MCP HTTP endpoint (Streamable HTTP, spec 2024-11-05) at /api/mcp/[token]
- Gmail OAuth connect/callback flow with AES-256-GCM token encryption
- Tools: send_email, create_draft via Gmail REST API
- Dashboard My MCPs tab with InstallModal, McpCard, SourceViewer, ConfigSnippet
- DB migration: installed_mcps table with RLS, indexes, and trigger

Co-Authored-By: Claude Sonnet 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

commit.message:
Add tab group keyboard navigation to ruler flyout

Three tab groups (zoom toolbar, radio list, checkbox/freq) with roving
tabindex, arrow key navigation within groups, Tab cycling between groups,
and stopPropagation to prevent track focus movement while flyout is open.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

repository.name: audacity-design-system
repository.description: Next.js prototype for the Audacity clip envelopes feature

commit.message:
Refactor compat checker: tuiCheck, multi-addon support, CDM conflicts

- Each conflict def now has tuiCheck (skip if our module is disabled) and
  tuiDisable (generic disable callback, no more hardcoded if/elseif)
- Support multiple competing addons per feature via addons array
- Dynamic popup text generated from detected addon label + category
- Add cooldownManager conflict: Ayije CDM, ArcUI CDM, BCDM, CDM Centered
- CDM init now guarded with IsCompatBlocked('cooldownManager')
- Eltruism: tuiAccept disables just their glow feature instead of the
  entire addon; externalCheck only fires if their glow is enabled

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>
